Una joya. La riquísima historia inca reflejada en cada rincón del santuario. Fácil de recorrer. Ideal para esa foto que no debe faltar en tu álbum. Recomiendo hacerlo con uno de los guías que ofrecen sus servicios web la entrada para que la experiencia cobre el sentido histórico que tienen las ruinas.

This is my third time here and let me tell you there’s been tons of changes. After you get to Aguas Calientes, everyone whether you hired a guide or not must now go to the Ministerio de Culturas and stand in line just to get a number to return later that night or the next day. My understanding from talking to other tour guides is that tour guides used to be able to buy your tickets and they would buy at the Peruvian National price and resell at the international tourist price. To cut down on this corruption now everyone must go and present their passports or their proof of residency (DNI). You are asked how many people in your party and you are given individual numbers for each person. You cannot show up early as they go by the number you were given. Their computer systems are not very stable so expect delays. We were in line a little over 2 hours in the morning and over 3 hours in the evening so almost 6 hours. When your number is called, you go upstairs and have to wait in and move yourself along the winding chairs that lead you back down the stairs to the cashier windows to be asked what circuit you will be getting. There are limited number of tickets per circuit and even though there are “maps” there is nothing showing you what you will see in each circuit and what your pictures will look like. There are screens that can be looked to determine which circuits are still available as you approach the window to place your order. Fee per permit/ticket is $50/person for international traveler regardless of the circuit chosen. Circuit 2A/B is quite popular so for me, I had to go with circuit 3A and that’s the picture I am sharing on this review. Previously on my other 2 visits, I was able to do the whole trip as a day trip and see Machu Picchu and return to Cusco the same day. Now you must stay overnight and some people up to two nights so plan carefully. That said, this area is a lower altitude than Cusco so it is easier to breathe and the town is much more quaint and more enjoyable. That said do not buy your return tickets to Cusco until you have your tour time because you are only allowed to make changes up to 24 hours before and if you try to change at 23 hours and 59 minutes, you will have to buy new tickets. Another piece of advice, when booking bus/train tickets go with Peru Rail instead of Inca Rail as the train cars are much nicer and have wider aisles and better “shows”. With these changes, it almost makes it not worth it to even visit. To me, Machu Picchu is completely overrated and overpriced and inconvenient to get to. Although I’m glad I’ve come 3 times (first time 2021 during covid and raining so pictures were not adequate, second time 2023 with niece and third time 2025 with my wife because she wanted to visit), I’ve thought this every time I’ve come.

Deberían informar a los visitantes previo a ello.

Nuestra bella Plaza de Lima, aquí vienen muchos turistas de distintas partes del mundo, a disfrutar de su belleza arquitectonica como sus iglesias que podrías visitar en todos sus alrededores y la catedral que es una de las más grandes, también ven con la barriga vacía porque podrás saciar tu paladar de deliciosos postres peruanos, además si vienes antes del medio día, podrás ver el cambio de guardia. Y si te animas también podrás tomar un tour hacia puntos turísticos muy cercanos por un precio módico.

La más popular quizá de las playas de paracas pero en lo personal no me agrado porque justamente es tan popular que cuando fui estaba totalmente lleno, parecía ya una feria y no me agrado por eso, el circuito litoral de esta playa es bonito, tiene una zona de arena y otra de piedras, unos metros mar adentro hay un montículo que sobresale desde el cual los bañistas hacen sus clavados; si se puede pasar un buen rato pero quizá en días particulares o en horarios con menos bullicio o menos carga turística...

It was nice, not even close to my favorite thing in Peru or my favorite trek but I would say definitely worth two to three days if you have time and are in the arequipa area. It is a very developed area for tourism so expect it to be busy and to have to pay for camping. I was recommended to take 5 litres of water, I took 6, and I did drink around 4, the reason I am included this however is that there were multiple locations where I could have refilled with my filter but didn't need to, therefore unless you drink a very large amount of water I wouldn't bring 6 litres. This is of course in the part of the canyon I did which started near cabanaconde.

A wonder del mundo, Machu Picchu is worth the long journey (flight to Lima, another flight to Cusco, taxi ride to Ollantaytambo, train ride to Aguas Calientes, and sunrise bus up to the entrance of Machu Picchu). Note that there are many prohibito items and there will be multiple pasaporte and boleto checks at the entrance and near the beginning of the path. Circuito 2 with the Inca bridge has an additional ranger patrolling the trail to the bridge, with a sign-in and sign-out process to control the number of visitors in the narrow area. A guide is technically mandatory for the entire park but is not rigidly enforced. However, it is recommended to arrange a trusted guia from the hotel rather than randomly picking one at the parada de autobus or entrada. My private guide charged 150 soles total for two people ($40 USD) and spent 3.5 hours from meeting at my hotel until leaving us at the area close to the salida (allowing us to stay and watch the llamas). Guides can help to take pictures of their clients at prime spots while explaining key details of the archeological history. Other than Inca bridge, there is no enforcement of the 4-hour total time limit through the complete circuit, though technically there is a one-way direction. Beware of biting insects (I washed my hands after applying a spray at the hotel and I received multiple bites from sand flies). The temperature is cool before sunrise but once the sun breaks through the fog and the view of the mountain is clear, the sunlight is bright and powerful. It's important to dress in layers and watch every step!

This place is very very expansive and not worse the money, the entrace is 152 sol. If you take a train its cost 65 dollars and bus to machu picchu is 24 dollars. ->If you want to save money<- We make everything without the train and bus. You need to arrive to santa teresa then to the electric station and then walk like 8 km to the entrace and then more like 1.5 km to the top of machu picchu. Buy tickets before or you will need to go the agua calientes and buy there(more 40 minuts walk). Total trip taked us 20 km.

1) YOU CANNOT WALK BACK, OR REPEAT ANY PART OF YOUR CIRCUIT. There are staff watching and they will blow their whistle and shout at you if you try. We arrived in a downpour so we walked to some shelter and waited it out. When we tried to walk back on the circuit we had paid for, we were not allowed. It doesn't matter that it's not busy, you want to take photos or any other reason, they will not let you walk back, even 20m. Unfortunately this was not clear when we purchased the ticket so we missed a significant portion of our route :( Therefore I'd advise you to walk slowly and enjoy the views, because once you arrive at the exit, that will be the end of your time at macchu pichu. I feel the staff lack compassion since it was 7am and quiet at macchu pichu, and I didn't want it to rain. If they had a cafe inside at the beginning I'd have waited there, but you have to enter at your time slot so I was left in a downpour in my poncho. I'd paid for flights, accomodation, and so many buses and trains to be there. The staff didn't show any compassion for my situation and were curt. <- therefore one star, but then I added one star for the staff that let me go take pictures of the llamas :) 2) TOILETS WILL COST YOU EXTRA ON TOP OF THE ENTRANCE FEE, AND BAGGAGE STORAGE. Which I also find ridiculous considering the entrance ticket is so expensive. Toilet S/2 and baggage is S/5. 3) YOU CANNOT ENTER BEFORE THE ENTRANCE TIME ON YOUR TICKET Something to do with the system they use. 4) DO NOT FLY A DRONE The staff have a walkie talkie system and respond very quickly. I don't know what the consequences are, but the people we saw were taken to the office. 5) DO NOT BOOK EARLY MORNING TIME SLOT IF YOU ARE VISITING IN WET/ SHOULDER SEASONS The clouds tend to hang low in the sky in the morning so you might not have the best view/ you might have to wait for clouds to clear. 6) YOU CAN BUY THE BUS TICKET FOR THE BUS UPWARDS ONLINE, BEFOREHAND (12 USD per ride) Just look for the one bus company that runs the route. I can't remember off the top of my head. You can also purchase them at the bottom and at the top, the process is quick and easy. Hope this helps :) I did route 1&2 and was happy with all the areas it covered.

It doesn't matter that it's not busy, you want to take photos or any other reason, they will not let you walk back, even 20m. Unfortunately this was not clear when we purchased the ticket so we missed a significant portion of our route :( Therefore I'd advise you to walk slowly and enjoy the views, because once you arrive at the exit, that will be the end of your time at macchu pichu. I feel the staff lack compassion since it was 7am and quiet at macchu pichu, and I didn't want it to rain. If they had a cafe inside at the beginning I'd have waited there, but you have to enter at your time slot so I was left in a downpour in my poncho. I'd paid for flights, accomodation, and so many buses and trains to be there. The staff didn't show any compassion for my situation and were curt. <- therefore one star, but then I added one star for the staff that let me go take pictures of the llamas :) 2) TOILETS WILL COST YOU EXTRA ON TOP OF THE ENTRANCE FEE, AND BAGGAGE STORAGE. Which I also find ridiculous considering the entrance ticket is so expensive. Toilet S/2 and baggage is S/5. 3) YOU CANNOT ENTER BEFORE THE ENTRANCE TIME ON YOUR TICKET Something to do with the system they use. 4) DO NOT FLY A DRONE The staff have a walkie talkie system and respond very quickly. I don't know what the consequences are, but the people we saw were taken to the office. 5) DO NOT BOOK EARLY MORNING TIME SLOT IF YOU ARE VISITING IN WET/ SHOULDER SEASONS The clouds tend to hang low in the sky in the morning so you might not have the best view/ you might have to wait for clouds to clear. 6) YOU CAN BUY THE BUS TICKET FOR THE BUS UPWARDS ONLINE, BEFOREHAND (12 USD per ride) Just look for the one bus company that runs the route. I can't remember off the top of my head. You can also purchase them at the bottom and at the top, the process is quick and easy. Hope this helps :) I did route 1&2 and was happy with all the areas it covered.

El cañon del colca tiene un paisaje imponente e inmenso, mucha flora y fauna. Hay que ir con buen calzado y un nivel intermedio de estado físico ya que hay que hacer trekking en altura y resulta medio dificultoso si no estás acostumbrado. Es viaje es bastante extenso, más o menos son 2 o 3 horas para llegar. No tuve la suerte de ver ningún Cóndor pero fue una linda experiencia. Esta excursión no la recomiendo hacer si vas a Perú con los días contados, hay lugares mejores.

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Hoy descubrí la historia del señor de sipán El museo un amplio complejo muy agradable con una zona trasera con casetas de artesanos La entrada se paga al entrar al recinto, para meter el coche son dos soles y la entrada de adultos son 10 soles No se puede acceder ni con móviles, cámaras ni bolsos al museo. Las taquillas las tienen en el exterior( cosa poco usual y de primeras un poco desconcertante, en fin) Puedes pagar por un guia 30soles. El museo se divide en tres plantas, no es muy grande se ve en una hora aproximadamente. Lo peor que vi es la oscuridad que tiene el museo. Las vitrinas con texto no se puede leer bien.

That said, I can't fully recommend heading this far into the highlands just to visit this site - it is certainly interesting, quiet and something different, not an essential visit on a travel schedule though.

Si puedes ve con un guía, ellos te explican la historia del monasterio de una manera muy dinámica (la guía Claudia es muy buena relatando). Es un lugar muy pintoresco por los colores de las calles, la arquitectura es curiosa, el arte hermoso y la vista desde la cúpula es genial. Dato para los estudiantes, lleven su carnet universitario para pagar la entrada de 12 soles. Adentro venden artesanías desde joyería hasta una crema de pétalos de rosa preparada por las monjas. Además cuentan con una cafetería con precios un poco alto.

15 soles entry. Some good production value has been out into the exhibition room with atmospheric music and cool artifacts with good explanations in English. The tour itself was great and our tour guide had great English (included in the ticket price). He explained about not only the archaeological site itself but showed us Peruvian/Liman fruit, vegetables and animals. Some good examples set up with mannequins to explain the way it was built and about their culture.

Una advertencia muy seria y real es que acá casi todo turista que visita Perú llega a este sitio que no es nada barato empezando con lo absurdamente caro que es el Tren que es el único medio (práctico y rápido) para llegar a Aguascalientes cuyo pasaje de ida y vuelta fácilmente te gastas 160 dólares, la entrada se tiene que comprar en línea con al menos 40 días de anticipación y el transporte de subida y bajada a las ruinas cuesta 24 dólares, no se gastarán menos de 220 dólares, lo terrible de esto es que el lugar siempre está atestado de gente se hacen largas filas para comprar pasajes de el bus, para subirse en el para regresarse, fila para entrar y ahí se te pueden ir fácilmente 4 horas si 4 horas, el clima es traiciónero en cualquier momento llueve y te frusta meses de planeación, por otro lados la Cuidad Perdida es simplemente subreal 😍

Eh. Overhyped and underwhelming. I did get engaged here though and I'm happy about that but the water/lights show was poorly edited together and the music didn't really go with the visuals. Laid out to make sure you get around to everything but since it's the holidays, it was a bit crowded. I'd say go earlier in the evening, when it's just starting to get dark. Inexpensive to get in so it's accessible to everyone.

Las telecabinas son de última generación, es un paseo bastante interesante. Pero la fortaleza está aún muy lejos de ser un punto turístico como Machu Picchu. Hay que caminar sobre tierra, rocas y lodo para poder llegar a la cima en donde verán la fortaleza desde afuera porque no se puede ingresar al complejo. La caminata dura aproximadamente 2 horas y es muy difícil para niños y adultos mayores, incluso personas que no tienen mucha resistencia física.
Maravillosa naturaleza. Ideal para caminar (a pesar de la altura) y disfrutar del paisaje.

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Imperdible visita si haces turismo en Chiclayo. Cerca del centro de la ciudad, estacionamiento y bastante orden. Si deseas conocer a fondo la historia de los diferentes señores que gobernaron está región en la época precolombina, es necesario contratar el servicio de un guía, disponibles al ingreso. Podrás ver todo tipo de piezas desde vasijas hasta impresionantes piezas de oro. Además replicas de las tumbas encontradas, entre otras maravillas. Digno de ver al menos una vez en la vida

My rating and comments are not related to the Machu Picchu itself (which is definitely one of the wonder of the world) but by the way visits are organized. It has unfortunately turned into a tourist zoo -it’s seem to be more daily tourists than the theoretical 2500 tickets allowance -visit must follow a one way buckle path, forget about following your inspiration to walk around -you must cross and queue within an horrible and crowded tourist village located around the train station -be also ready to queue within train station, to get on board the bus to go down, to enter the sight... -and obviously to contribute generously to Peruvian government budget

Lugar interesante. Solo se puede recorrer con la guía turística del lugar, por lo que uno al llegar puede que tenga que esperar hasta que salga algún grupo. Hay una sala similar a un museo que explica todo tal cual lo hace el guía, por lo que resulta redundante la guía turística, más cuando son algo monótonas y uno está a pleno rayo del sol. Se nota que devastaron el lugar, pues la ciudad lo fue arruinando. Fue algo decepcionante ver obreros refaccionando la pirámide cual antiguos creadores de la misma

text_clean: We loved it. Went to Pisco airport (local airport), took about 50 min to get to site, then the roller coaster ride began. They bank turn, bank left to allow passengers on both sides to see it. We went with Aerodiana, nice plane in good condition. Lucky I read about the other unsafe airlines and emergency landings after my ride or would have been a nervous wreck during the flight. Was lucky had a beautiful sunny clear day and smooth ride (aside from the purpose banking, poor kid on our plane threw up. The plane had plenty of bags for situations like these 😂)

El lugar no tiene facilidades para personas con movilidad limitada. Adicionalmente, una guía llamada Saskia Prieto fue discriminante con una turista porque la turista no pudo caminar a la velocidad de su grupo y se retiró para poder avanzar sola a su propio paso. La turista fue maltratada y sin respetar sus derechos ni sus limitaciones esta mala guía, obligó a la turista a salir del lugar humillándola! Es un acto discriminatorio! Será que las personas de movilidad limitada no tenemos derecho para visitar este lugar histórico???

text_clean: Evidentemente son dibujadas desde y para el aire Pero lo que a mi me demuestra su origen superior es la increible calida de los diseños Soy profesor de historia de la arquitectura y he intentado que mis alumnos dibujen cosas asi. Y nopueden acercarse a esa calidad de los seres un poco superiores que los hicieron. Al igual que los dibujos de los campos de todo el mundo hechos por seres un poquito mas matematicos y creativos que nosotros Las lineas de Nazca son un juego La estacion del mono o del colibri Los aliens son muy ludicos su hermosa vida superior lo hace asi Otra prueba desu origen se puede ver en el mar donde con gogle se puede ver que tambien las hicieron alli Arq.Raúl Cordero Ecuador

Arequipa is an incredible experience overall and surprisingly affordable! Known as the White City, Arequipa boasts charming architecture and a vibrant culture that makes every visit special. One of the highlights of my trip was visiting the breathtaking Colca Canyon. The landscapes of Colca Canyon are absolutely unbelievable. The vast, rugged beauty of the canyon leaves you in awe, and it's a fantastic place to connect with nature. The tranquility and peacefulness you find there are unmatched. It's a perfect escape from the hustle and bustle of daily life. Walking along the canyon, you can truly appreciate the serene environment and the stunning views that stretch as far as the eye can see. Watching the majestic condors glide through the air is an experience in itself. Arequipa adds to the experience with its unique blend of historical sites and modern amenities. The combination of the city's unique charm and the breathtaking beauty of Colca Canyon makes for a memorable visit. Whether you're hiking the trails, soaking in the hot springs, or simply taking in the views, it's a place that invites you to relax and unwind. Overall, visiting Colca Canyon in Arequipa is an unforgettable experience that I highly recommend. The natural beauty, peaceful atmosphere, and affordability make it a must-see destination for anyone looking to explore the wonders of Peru.

Cuando este parque se creó incluía poder disfrutar de todas las piletas por una sola entrada. El alcalde que lo diseñó pensó en el relajo de la rutina y un regalo a la población. Ahora cobran doble por ver todas las piletas con la excusa de hacer un espectáculo temático. Aparte de ser terrible, al haber quitado áreas verdes para poner insoportables juegos desmerece la obra. Qué tal desproposito cumple esta obra ahora. Espero puedan considerar regresar el diseño del parque a sus orígenes.

Fui al circuito 1-C el 17 de abril a las 8:00 am y me tocó lluvia y niebla. No obstante despeja por momentos y se puede apreciar la vista desde la casa del guardian al que se tiene acceso, pero por la condición climática descarté la subida que ofrecía la ruta porque no valía la pena. Mejor reservar para entrar al medio día, vas a la fija. Aunque el paisaje es encantador por las montañas, incluso desde el tren, me pareció que tanto sacrificio y dinero para llegar hasta allí está un poco sobrevalorado. Demasiados turistas.

Realmente muy costoso. Subir en bus en precio para latinos es un abuso, para un tramo de 20 minutos. Les recomiendo lleguuen muy antes a cusco y suban caminando. Una vez arriba es bonita la vista pero los guardias dentro del lugar son GROSEROS, ALTANEROS. Vivi una pesima experiencia gracias a ellos. Recuerdo que con mis compañeros hicimos un tiktok donde nos veíamos emocionados de estar alla. Y llego un tipo de estos diciendo que borraramos el video que me iba a decomisar el celular. Que esta prohibido grabar videos unas cosas super estupidas. Hicieron de la estancia una pésima experiencia. … no pagaría jamas por volver. Hay lugares mil veces mejores y mil veces mas economicos y sin estos tipos.

Machu Picchu itself is certainly an impressive archeological site. The price (entry ticket per circuit 152S, resp. 50$) is very high in my regards, especially since there is zero information given on the site itself. Further all means of transport are overpriced compared to the rest of Peru and there is no alternative. Overall a nice experience, but felt overpriced, without any reasonable alternative for means of transport, too little/no information and thus a overrated.

Una de las maravillas del mundo Antiguo Machu Picchu es una de las joyas arqueológicas mas espectaculares del mundo 🤩. La energía de este lugar es increíble 😊 Recomiendo: - Hacer las reservaciones para las 11:00 ya que es una hora estratégica y no hay mucha aglomeración de personas y el sol no es tan intenso. ☀️ - Llevar mucha agua 💦 porque el recorrido es largo y el calor es intenso🥵 - Llevar bloqueador solar 🧴 ☀️ - Obligatoriamente llevar repelente de insectos 🦟 ya qué hay muchos la mosquitos 🦟 y sus picaduras son muy dolorosas. - Llevar un poncho de lluvia 🌧 - Después del recorrido tomate un Cuzqueña 🍻

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Un museo muy bonito, sin embargo es muy frustrante que no te dejen tomar fotos dentro de este. Tiene tres pisos, empiezas por el tercero y vas bajando, te muestran no sólo la tumba del señor de sipan, sino también otras tumbas que se encontraron dentro de la misma huaca, sus métodos de sepultura, la cerámica de los mochica y su cosmovisión (dioses).

We weren't sure if it would fit in our schedule for Peru at first, but I'm very glad we made a detour and planned some extra time for this. Absolutely stunning views and very varied flora and fauna. A few notes: the local authority Autocolca doesn't do their bookkeeping very well and I don't recommend getting E-tickets. Get your tickets in the early morning from their office in Chivay (plaza des armas) as you have the best chance of spotting the mighty condor in the morning, and they don't do much work there in the afternoon. Also I do not recommend going over the S109 (south south west) unless you have plenty of time (travel time with google maps is spot-on though) a sturdy car and good driving skills, because the road is undergoing about 100kms of heavy construction with poor driving conditions.

I came to Arequipa to enjoy the city and to visit the Monastery was something I was excited about doing. I arrived yesterday and I was extremely disappointed to find that there was no way for someone like me to enter and enjoy this beautiful piece of architecture and history.. I have muscular dystrophy and to use stairs is basically impossible for me. I understand that it is a historic city and things sometimes can’t be changed for preservation purposes - but to have no ramp or mini platform elevator or alternate entrance or even a handrail for the stairs was shocking and disappointing to say the least. I only write this review to let others know who may be physically altered (senior citizens or pregnant women) or handicapped like me that this will be what you find when you arrive there. It makes no sense for a place that is so historical and beautiful and important, a tourist treasure of the city, to not be accesible to everyone. I was still able to enjoy some of the beautiful architecture from the outside, but it was very sad not to be able to experience this.

Disappointing :-( First of all, my experience has nothing to do with the actual site. The view, ruins, landscape and llamas are surreal! My disappointing experience is about the management of the site. I knew it was a touristy place and quite expensive to go. Most places are restricted/controlled and you can't enter inside the temples etc..you can just go around. Apparently a few years ago everything was open (said some friends who went before). And then you have all the tour groups that take a lot of spaces on the path. I almost didn't take any pictures, I didn't feel I had the freedom to do so because the path goes only on one way (so you can't go back on your path) and people are moving so you kind of feel a bit in a tour group without being in it and you kind of have to go forward. I didn't feel free to walk around/take pictures as I would have liked to. And then there are 4 choices of tickets, with different circuits, bit it doesn't give you access to everywhere with only one ticket, so you need to buy another one. This wasn't very cleared on the website. I just find that you put so much effort (physically, mentally and financially) to get there (waking up super early, doing all the trip to get to Aguas Calientes, walking up the (many!) stairs to the entrance, and the cost etc), in the end to leave with a bitter feeling. Is it worth it? I'm still unsure if it was.

I really hate 1 star reviews for this historical site but I am writing one. Due to the protests in September of 2025 we were unable to get our train to Aguas Caliente. I completely support people's right to protest and was unwilling to travel to MP during this dispute. Some people travelled by van for 5 hours and walked for several more miles to access the town. I chose to stay in Ollantaytambo for a couple of extra days which was really enjoyable. I am giving MP 1 star because despite the issues people have accessing MP their refund process is impossible. I was told I need to submit personal information including pictures of my passport. They sent me a link to do this. The link does not say anything about the purpose of why I am submitting this information. I am wary of providing this information without a guarantee of getting a refund. The ticketing website has no information about asking for refunds. Surely they are aware that thousands of people were unable to go to MP due to the strike. Perurail actually sent an email explaining the refund process. I imagine the ministry of tourism made quite a bit of money from no-show tickets.

Esperaba mucho más. La entrada carisima, no incluía el guía. El mobiliario de la época estaba deteriorado y en muy mal estado. Otros claustros estaban en mejores condiciones y con un costo de ingreso por debajo de la mitad de este. Espero que sea transitorio para regresar y ver en mejor estado este monumento histórico.

Una joya. La riquísima historia inca reflejada en cada rincón del santuario. Fácil de recorrer. Ideal para esa foto que no debe faltar en tu álbum. Recomiendo hacerlo con uno de los guías que ofrecen sus servicios web la entrada para que la experiencia cobre el sentido histórico que tienen las ruinas.

This is my third time here and let me tell you there’s been tons of changes. After you get to Aguas Calientes, everyone whether you hired a guide or not must now go to the Ministerio de Culturas and stand in line just to get a number to return later that night or the next day. My understanding from talking to other tour guides is that tour guides used to be able to buy your tickets and they would buy at the Peruvian National price and resell at the international tourist price. To cut down on this corruption now everyone must go and present their passports or their proof of residency (DNI). You are asked how many people in your party and you are given individual numbers for each person. You cannot show up early as they go by the number you were given. Their computer systems are not very stable so expect delays. We were in line a little over 2 hours in the morning and over 3 hours in the evening so almost 6 hours. When your number is called, you go upstairs and have to wait in and move yourself along the winding chairs that lead you back down the stairs to the cashier windows to be asked what circuit you will be getting. There are limited number of tickets per circuit and even though there are “maps” there is nothing showing you what you will see in each circuit and what your pictures will look like. There are screens that can be looked to determine which circuits are still available as you approach the window to place your order. Fee per permit/ticket is $50/person for international traveler regardless of the circuit chosen. Circuit 2A/B is quite popular so for me, I had to go with circuit 3A and that’s the picture I am sharing on this review. Previously on my other 2 visits, I was able to do the whole trip as a day trip and see Machu Picchu and return to Cusco the same day. Now you must stay overnight and some people up to two nights so plan carefully. That said, this area is a lower altitude than Cusco so it is easier to breathe and the town is much more quaint and more enjoyable. That said do not buy your return tickets to Cusco until you have your tour time because you are only allowed to make changes up to 24 hours before and if you try to change at 23 hours and 59 minutes, you will have to buy new tickets. Another piece of advice, when booking bus/train tickets go with Peru Rail instead of Inca Rail as the train cars are much nicer and have wider aisles and better “shows”. With these changes, it almost makes it not worth it to even visit. To me, Machu Picchu is completely overrated and overpriced and inconvenient to get to. Although I’m glad I’ve come 3 times (first time 2021 during covid and raining so pictures were not adequate, second time 2023 with niece and third time 2025 with my wife because she wanted to visit), I’ve thought this every time I’ve come.

El recorrido es corto, lamentablemente para la fecha que fui estaban en reestructuración los accesos 1 y 2, parte del recorrido fue algo decepcionante ya que habían tablas y maderas de los lugares a reestructurar, por lo que considero pagar 30 soles el costo de una entrada cuando la fortaleza se encuentra en ese estado no es justo. Deberían informar a los visitantes previo a ello.

Nuestra bella Plaza de Lima, aquí vienen muchos turistas de distintas partes del mundo, a disfrutar de su belleza arquitectonica como sus iglesias que podrías visitar en todos sus alrededores y la catedral que es una de las más grandes, también ven con la barriga vacía porque podrás saciar tu paladar de deliciosos postres peruanos, además si vienes antes del medio día, podrás ver el cambio de guardia. Y si te animas también podrás tomar un tour hacia puntos turísticos muy cercanos por un precio módico.

La más popular quizá de las playas de paracas pero en lo personal no me agrado porque justamente es tan popular que cuando fui estaba totalmente lleno, parecía ya una feria y no me agrado por eso, el circuito litoral de esta playa es bonito, tiene una zona de arena y otra de piedras, unos metros mar adentro hay un montículo que sobresale desde el cual los bañistas hacen sus clavados; si se puede pasar un buen rato pero quizá en días particulares o en horarios con menos bullicio o menos carga turística...

It was nice, not even close to my favorite thing in Peru or my favorite trek but I would say definitely worth two to three days if you have time and are in the arequipa area. It is a very developed area for tourism so expect it to be busy and to have to pay for camping. I was recommended to take 5 litres of water, I took 6, and I did drink around 4, the reason I am included this however is that there were multiple locations where I could have refilled with my filter but didn't need to, therefore unless you drink a very large amount of water I wouldn't bring 6 litres. This is of course in the part of the canyon I did which started near cabanaconde.

A wonder del mundo, Machu Picchu is worth the long journey (flight to Lima, another flight to Cusco, taxi ride to Ollantaytambo, train ride to Aguas Calientes, and sunrise bus up to the entrance of Machu Picchu). Note that there are many prohibito items and there will be multiple pasaporte and boleto checks at the entrance and near the beginning of the path. Circuito 2 with the Inca bridge has an additional ranger patrolling the trail to the bridge, with a sign-in and sign-out process to control the number of visitors in the narrow area. A guide is technically mandatory for the entire park but is not rigidly enforced. However, it is recommended to arrange a trusted guia from the hotel rather than randomly picking one at the parada de autobus or entrada. My private guide charged 150 soles total for two people ($40 USD) and spent 3.5 hours from meeting at my hotel until leaving us at the area close to the salida (allowing us to stay and watch the llamas). Guides can help to take pictures of their clients at prime spots while explaining key details of the archeological history. Other than Inca bridge, there is no enforcement of the 4-hour total time limit through the complete circuit, though technically there is a one-way direction. Beware of biting insects (I washed my hands after applying a spray at the hotel and I received multiple bites from sand flies). The temperature is cool before sunrise but once the sun breaks through the fog and the view of the mountain is clear, the sunlight is bright and powerful. It's important to dress in layers and watch every step!

This place is very very expansive and not worse the money, the entrace is 152 sol. If you take a train its cost 65 dollars and bus to machu picchu is 24 dollars. ->If you want to save money<- We make everything without the train and bus. You need to arrive to santa teresa then to the electric station and then walk like 8 km to the entrace and then more like 1.5 km to the top of machu picchu. Buy tickets before or you will need to go the agua calientes and buy there(more 40 minuts walk). Total trip taked us 20 km.

1) YOU CANNOT WALK BACK, OR REPEAT ANY PART OF YOUR CIRCUIT. There are staff watching and they will blow their whistle and shout at you if you try. We arrived in a downpour so we walked to some shelter and waited it out. When we tried to walk back on the circuit we had paid for, we were not allowed. It doesn't matter that it's not busy, you want to take photos or any other reason, they will not let you walk back, even 20m. Unfortunately this was not clear when we purchased the ticket so we missed a significant portion of our route :( Therefore I'd advise you to walk slowly and enjoy the views, because once you arrive at the exit, that will be the end of your time at macchu pichu. I feel the staff lack compassion since it was 7am and quiet at macchu pichu, and I didn't want it to rain. If they had a cafe inside at the beginning I'd have waited there, but you have to enter at your time slot so I was left in a downpour in my poncho. I'd paid for flights, accomodation, and so many buses and trains to be there. The staff didn't show any compassion for my situation and were curt. <- therefore one star, but then I added one star for the staff that let me go take pictures of the llamas :) 2) TOILETS WILL COST YOU EXTRA ON TOP OF THE ENTRANCE FEE, AND BAGGAGE STORAGE. Which I also find ridiculous considering the entrance ticket is so expensive. Toilet S/2 and baggage is S/5. 3) YOU CANNOT ENTER BEFORE THE ENTRANCE TIME ON YOUR TICKET Something to do with the system they use. 4) DO NOT FLY A DRONE The staff have a walkie talkie system and respond very quickly. I don't know what the consequences are, but the people we saw were taken to the office. 5) DO NOT BOOK EARLY MORNING TIME SLOT IF YOU ARE VISITING IN WET/ SHOULDER SEASONS The clouds tend to hang low in the sky in the morning so you might not have the best view/ you might have to wait for clouds to clear. 6) YOU CAN BUY THE BUS TICKET FOR THE BUS UPWARDS ONLINE, BEFOREHAND (12 USD per ride) Just look for the one bus company that runs the route. I can't remember off the top of my head. You can also purchase them at the bottom and at the top, the process is quick and easy. Hope this helps :) I did route 1&2 and was happy with all the areas it covered.

1) YOU CANNOT WALK BACK, OR REPEAT ANY PART OF YOUR CIRCUIT. There are staff watching and they will blow their whistle and shout at you if you try. We arrived in a downpour so we walked to some shelter and waited it out. When we tried to walk back on the circuit we had paid for, we were not allowed. It doesn't matter that it's not busy, you want to take photos or any other reason, they will not let you walk back, even 20m. Unfortunately this was not clear when we purchased the ticket so we missed a significant portion of our route :( Therefore I'd advise you to walk slowly and enjoy the views, because once you arrive at the exit, that will be the end of your time at macchu pichu. I feel the staff lack compassion since it was 7am and quiet at macchu pichu, and I didn't want it to rain. El cañon del colca tiene un paisaje imponente e inmenso, mucha flora y fauna. Hay que ir con buen calzado y un nivel intermedio de estado físico ya que hay que hacer trekking en altura y resulta medio dificultoso si no estás acostumbrado. Es viaje es bastante extenso, más o menos son 2 o 3 horas para llegar. No tuve la suerte de ver ningún Cóndor pero fue una linda experiencia. Esta excursión no la recomiendo hacer si vas a Perú con los días contados, hay lugares mejores.

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Hoy descubrí la historia del señor de sipán El museo un amplio complejo muy agradable con una zona trasera con casetas de artesanos La entrada se paga al entrar al recinto, para meter el coche son dos soles y la entrada de adultos son 10 soles No se puede acceder ni con móviles, cámaras ni bolsos al museo. Las taquillas las tienen en el exterior( cosa poco usual y de primeras un poco desconcertante, en fin) Puedes pagar por un guia 30soles. El museo se divide en tres plantas, no es muy grande se ve en una hora aproximadamente. Lo peor que vi es la oscuridad que tiene el museo. Las vitrinas con texto no se puede leer bien.

A few people have compared this to Machu Pichu, but this is odd and inaccurate, these ruins should be approached on their own merit. That said, I can't fully recommend heading this far into the highlands just to visit this site - it is certainly interesting, quiet and something different, not an essential visit on a travel schedule though.

Si puedes ve con un guía, ellos te explican la historia del monasterio de una manera muy dinámica (la guía Claudia es muy buena relatando). Es un lugar muy pintoresco por los colores de las calles, la arquitectura es curiosa, el arte hermoso y la vista desde la cúpula es genial. Dato para los estudiantes, lleven su carnet universitario para pagar la entrada de 12 soles. Adentro venden artesanías desde joyería hasta una crema de pétalos de rosa preparada por las monjas. Además cuentan con una cafetería con precios un poco alto.

15 soles entry. Some good production value has been out into the exhibition room with atmospheric music and cool artifacts with good explanations in English. The tour itself was great and our tour guide had great English (included in the ticket price). He explained about not only the archaeological site itself but showed us Peruvian/Liman fruit, vegetables and animals. Some good examples set up with mannequins to explain the way it was built and about their culture.

Una advertencia muy seria y real es que acá casi todo turista que visita Perú llega a este sitio que no es nada barato empezando con lo absurdamente caro que es el Tren que es el único medio (práctico y rápido) para llegar a Aguascalientes cuyo pasaje de ida y vuelta fácilmente te gastas 160 dólares, la entrada se tiene que comprar en línea con al menos 40 días de anticipación y el transporte de subida y bajada a las ruinas cuesta 24 dólares, no se gastarán menos de 220 dólares, lo terrible de esto es que el lugar siempre está atestado de gente se hacen largas filas para comprar pasajes de el bus, para subirse en el para regresarse, fila para entrar y ahí se te pueden ir fácilmente 4 horas si 4 horas, el clima es traiciónero en cualquier momento llueve y te frusta meses de planeación, por otro lados la Cuidad Perdida es simplemente subreal 😍

Eh. Overhyped and underwhelming. I did get engaged here though and I'm happy about that but the water/lights show was poorly edited together and the music didn't really go with the visuals. Laid out to make sure you get around to everything but since it's the holidays, it was a bit crowded. I'd say go earlier in the evening, when it's just starting to get dark. Inexpensive to get in so it's accessible to everyone.

Las telecabinas son de última generación, es un paseo bastante interesante. Pero la fortaleza está aún muy lejos de ser un punto turístico como Machu Picchu. Hay que caminar sobre tierra, rocas y lodo para poder llegar a la cima en donde verán la fortaleza desde afuera porque no se puede ingresar al complejo. La caminata dura aproximadamente 2 horas y es muy difícil para niños y adultos mayores, incluso personas que no tienen mucha resistencia física.
Maravillosa naturaleza. Ideal para caminar (a pesar de la altura) y disfrutar del paisaje.

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Imperdible visita si haces turismo en Chiclayo. Cerca del centro de la ciudad, estacionamiento y bastante orden. Si deseas conocer a fondo la historia de los diferentes señores que gobernaron está región en la época precolombina, es necesario contratar el servicio de un guía, disponibles al ingreso. Podrás ver todo tipo de piezas desde vasijas hasta impresionantes piezas de oro. Además replicas de las tumbas encontradas, entre otras maravillas. Digno de ver al menos una vez en la vida

El recorrido nocturno es el mejor, un poco tenebroso ya que va poca gente y está ambientado con velas, mecheros y carbón. My rating and comments are not related to the Machu Picchu itself (which is definitely one of the wonder of the world) but by the way visits are organized. It has unfortunately turned into a tourist zoo -it’s seem to be more daily tourists than the theoretical 2500 tickets allowance -visit must follow a one way buckle path, forget about following your inspiration to walk around -you must cross and queue within an horrible and crowded tourist village located around the train station -be also ready to queue within train station, to get on board the bus to go down, to enter the sight... -and obviously to contribute generously to Peruvian government budget

Lugar interesante. Solo se puede recorrer con la guía turística del lugar, por lo que uno al llegar puede que tenga que esperar hasta que salga algún grupo. Hay una sala similar a un museo que explica todo tal cual lo hace el guía, por lo que resulta redundante la guía turística, más cuando son algo monótonas y uno está a pleno rayo del sol. Se nota que devastaron el lugar, pues la ciudad lo fue arruinando. Fue algo decepcionante ver obreros refaccionando la pirámide cual antiguos creadores de la misma

text_clean: We loved it. Went to Pisco airport (local airport), took about 50 min to get to site, then the roller coaster ride began. They bank turn, bank left to allow passengers on both sides to see it. We went with Aerodiana, nice plane in good condition. Lucky I read about the other unsafe airlines and emergency landings after my ride or would have been a nervous wreck during the flight. Was lucky had a beautiful sunny clear day and smooth ride (aside from the purpose banking, poor kid on our plane threw up. The plane had plenty of bags for situations like these 😂)

El lugar no tiene facilidades para personas con movilidad limitada. Adicionalmente, una guía llamada Saskia Prieto fue discriminante con una turista porque la turista no pudo caminar a la velocidad de su grupo y se retiró para poder avanzar sola a su propio paso. La turista fue maltratada y sin respetar sus derechos ni sus limitaciones esta mala guía, obligó a la turista a salir del lugar humillándola! Es un acto discriminatorio! Será que las personas de movilidad limitada no tenemos derecho para visitar este lugar histórico???

Y nopueden acercarse a esa calidad de los seres un poco superiores que los hicieron. Al igual que los dibujos de los campos de todo el mundo hechos por seres un poquito mas matematicos y creativos que nosotros Las lineas de Nazca son un juego La estacion del mono o del colibri Los aliens son muy ludicos su hermosa vida superior lo hace asi Otra prueba desu origen se puede ver en el mar donde con gogle se puede ver que tambien las hicieron alli Arq.Raúl Cordero Ecuador

Arequipa is an incredible experience overall and surprisingly affordable! Known as the White City, Arequipa boasts charming architecture and a vibrant culture that makes every visit special. One of the highlights of my trip was visiting the breathtaking Colca Canyon. The landscapes of Colca Canyon are absolutely unbelievable. The vast, rugged beauty of the canyon leaves you in awe, and it's a fantastic place to connect with nature. The tranquility and peacefulness you find there are unmatched. It's a perfect escape from the hustle and bustle of daily life. Walking along the canyon, you can truly appreciate the serene environment and the stunning views that stretch as far as the eye can see. Watching the majestic condors glide through the air is an experience in itself. Arequipa adds to the experience with its unique blend of historical sites and modern amenities. The combination of the city's unique charm and the breathtaking beauty of Colca Canyon makes for a memorable visit. Whether you're hiking the trails, soaking in the hot springs, or simply taking in the views, it's a place that invites you to relax and unwind. Overall, visiting Colca Canyon in Arequipa is an unforgettable experience that I highly recommend. The natural beauty, peaceful atmosphere, and affordability make it a must-see destination for anyone looking to explore the wonders of Peru.

Cuando este parque se creó incluía poder disfrutar de todas las piletas por una sola entrada. El alcalde que lo diseñó pensó en el relajo de la rutina y un regalo a la población. Ahora cobran doble por ver todas las piletas con la excusa de hacer un espectáculo temático. Aparte de ser terrible, al haber quitado áreas verdes para poner insoportables juegos desmerece la obra. Qué tal desproposito cumple esta obra ahora. Espero puedan considerar regresar el diseño del parque a sus orígenes.

Fui al circuito 1-C el 17 de abril a las 8:00 am y me tocó lluvia y niebla. No obstante despeja por momentos y se puede apreciar la vista desde la casa del guardian al que se tiene acceso, pero por la condición climática descarté la subida que ofrecía la ruta porque no valía la pena. Mejor reservar para entrar al medio día, vas a la fija. Aunque el paisaje es encantador por las montañas, incluso desde el tren, me pareció que tanto sacrificio y dinero para llegar hasta allí está un poco sobrevalorado. Demasiados turistas.

Realmente muy costoso. Subir en bus en precio para latinos es un abuso, para un tramo de 20 minutos. Les recomiendo lleguuen muy antes a cusco y suban caminando. Una vez arriba es bonita la vista pero los guardias dentro del lugar son GROSEROS, ALTANEROS. Vivi una pesima experiencia gracias a ellos. Recuerdo que con mis compañeros hicimos un tiktok donde nos veíamos emocionados de estar alla. Y llego un tipo de estos diciendo que borraramos el video que me iba a decomisar el celular. Que esta prohibido grabar videos unas cosas super estupidas. Hicieron de la estancia una pésima experiencia. … no pagaría jamas por volver. Hay lugares mil veces mejores y mil veces mas economicos y sin estos tipos.

Machu Picchu itself is certainly an impressive archeological site. The price (entry ticket per circuit 152S, resp. 50$) is very high in my regards, especially since there is zero information given on the site itself. Further all means of transport are overpriced compared to the rest of Peru and there is no alternative. Overall a nice experience, but felt overpriced, without any reasonable alternative for means of transport, too little/no information and thus a overrated.

Una de las maravillas del mundo Antiguo Machu Picchu es una de las joyas arqueológicas mas espectaculares del mundo 🤩. La energía de este lugar es increíble 😊 Recomiendo: - Hacer las reservaciones para las 11:00 ya que es una hora estratégica y no hay mucha aglomeración de personas y el sol no es tan intenso. ☀️ - Llevar mucha agua 💦 porque el recorrido es largo y el calor es intenso🥵 - Llevar bloqueador solar 🧴 ☀️ - Obligatoriamente llevar repelente de insectos 🦟 ya qué hay muchos la mosquitos 🦟 y sus picaduras son muy dolorosas. - Llevar un poncho de lluvia 🌧 - Después del recorrido tomate un Cuzqueña 🍻

Dependiendo que tanto demoren en ver cada lugar y su ritmo de caminata, el ticket solo permite 3 horas de visita ya que si no, tendrán que pagar de nuevo el ingreso a telecabina, el tiempo de visita a la ciudadela es ilimitado pero estar atento al ticket de la telecabina.

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Un museo muy bonito, sin embargo es muy frustrante que no te dejen tomar fotos dentro de este. Tiene tres pisos, empiezas por el tercero y vas bajando, te muestran no sólo la tumba del señor de sipan, sino también otras tumbas que se encontraron dentro de la misma huaca, sus métodos de sepultura, la cerámica de los mochica y su cosmovisión (dioses).

We weren't sure if it would fit in our schedule for Peru at first, but I'm very glad we made a detour and planned some extra time for this. Absolutely stunning views and very varied flora and fauna. A few notes: the local authority Autocolca doesn't do their bookkeeping very well and I don't recommend getting E-tickets. Get your tickets in the early morning from their office in Chivay (plaza des armas) as you have the best chance of spotting the mighty condor in the morning, and they don't do much work there in the afternoon. Also I do not recommend going over the S109 (south south west) unless you have plenty of time (travel time with google maps is spot-on though) a sturdy car and good driving skills, because the road is undergoing about 100kms of heavy construction with poor driving conditions.

I came to Arequipa to enjoy the city and to visit the Monastery was something I was excited about doing. I arrived yesterday and I was extremely disappointed to find that there was no way for someone like me to enter and enjoy this beautiful piece of architecture and history.. I have muscular dystrophy and to use stairs is basically impossible for me. I understand that it is a historic city and things sometimes can’t be changed for preservation purposes - but to have no ramp or mini platform elevator or alternate entrance or even a handrail for the stairs was shocking and disappointing to say the least. I only write this review to let others know who may be physically altered (senior citizens or pregnant women) or handicapped like me that this will be what you find when you arrive there. It makes no sense for a place that is so historical and beautiful and important, a tourist treasure of the city, to not be accesible to everyone. I was still able to enjoy some of the beautiful architecture from the outside, but it was very sad not to be able to experience this.

Disappointing :-( First of all, my experience has nothing to do with the actual site. The view, ruins, landscape and llamas are surreal! My disappointing experience is about the management of the site. I knew it was a touristy place and quite expensive to go. Most places are restricted/controlled and you can't enter inside the temples etc..you can just go around. Apparently a few years ago everything was open (said some friends who went before). And then you have all the tour groups that take a lot of spaces on the path. I almost didn't take any pictures, I didn't feel I had the freedom to do so because the path goes only on one way (so you can't go back on your path) and people are moving so you kind of feel a bit in a tour group without being in it and you kind of have to go forward. I didn't feel free to walk around/take pictures as I would have liked to. And then there are 4 choices of tickets, with different circuits, bit it doesn't give you access to everywhere with only one ticket, so you need to buy another one. This wasn't very cleared on the website. I just find that you put so much effort (physically, mentally and financially) to get there (waking up super early, doing all the trip to get to Aguas Calientes, walking up the (many!) stairs to the entrance, and the cost etc), in the end to leave with a bitter feeling. Is it worth it? I'm still unsure if it was.

I really hate 1 star reviews for this historical site but I am writing one. Due to the protests in September of 2025 we were unable to get our train to Aguas Caliente. I completely support people's right to protest and was unwilling to travel to MP during this dispute. Some people travelled by van for 5 hours and walked for several more miles to access the town. I chose to stay in Ollantaytambo for a couple of extra days which was really enjoyable. I am giving MP 1 star because despite the issues people have accessing MP their refund process is impossible. I was told I need to submit personal information including pictures of my passport. They sent me a link to do this. The link does not say anything about the purpose of why I am submitting this information. I am wary of providing this information without a guarantee of getting a refund. The ticketing website has no information about asking for refunds. Surely they are aware that thousands of people were unable to go to MP due to the strike. Perurail actually sent an email explaining the refund process. I imagine the ministry of tourism made quite a bit of money from no-show tickets.

This is my third time here and let me tell you there’s been tons of changes. After you get to Aguas Calientes, everyone whether you hired a guide or not must now go to the Ministerio de Culturas and stand in line just to get a number to return later that night or the next day. My understanding from talking to other tour guides is that tour guides used to be able to buy your tickets and they would buy at the Peruvian National price and resell at the international tourist price. To cut down on this corruption now everyone must go and present their passports or their proof of residency (DNI). You are asked how many people in your party and you are given individual numbers for each person. You cannot show up early as they go by the number you were given. Their computer systems are not very stable so expect delays. We were in line a little over 2 hours in the morning and over 3 hours in the evening so almost 6 hours. When your number is called, you go upstairs and have to wait in and move yourself along the winding chairs that lead you back down the stairs to the cashier windows to be asked what circuit you will be getting. There are limited number of tickets per circuit and even though there are “maps” there is nothing showing you what you will see in each circuit and what your pictures will look like. There are screens that can be looked to determine which circuits are still available as you approach the window to place your order. Fee per permit/ticket is $50/person for international traveler regardless of the circuit chosen. Circuit 2A/B is quite popular so for me, I had to go with circuit 3A and that’s the picture I am sharing on this review. Previously on my other 2 visits, I was able to do the whole trip as a day trip and see Machu Picchu and return to Cusco the same day. Now you must stay overnight and some people up to two nights so plan carefully. That said, this area is a lower altitude than Cusco so it is easier to breathe and the town is much more quaint and more enjoyable. That said do not buy your return tickets to Cusco until you have your tour time because you are only allowed to make changes up to 24 hours before and if you try to change at 23 hours and 59 minutes, you will have to buy new tickets. Another piece of advice, when booking bus/train tickets go with Peru Rail instead of Inca Rail as the train cars are much nicer and have wider aisles and better “shows”. With these changes, it almost makes it not worth it to even visit. To me, Machu Picchu is completely overrated and overpriced and inconvenient to get to. Although I’m glad I’ve come 3 times (first time 2021 during covid and raining so pictures were not adequate, second time 2023 with niece and third time 2025 with my wife because she wanted to visit), I’ve thought this every time I’ve come.

With these changes, it almost makes it not worth it to even visit. To me, Machu Picchu is completely overrated and overpriced and inconvenient to get to. Although I’m glad I’ve come 3 times (first time 2021 during covid and raining so pictures were not adequate, second time 2023 with niece and third time 2025 with my wife because she wanted to visit), I’ve thought this every time I’ve come.

This is my third time here and let me tell you there’s been tons of changes. After you get to Aguas Calientes, everyone whether you hired a guide or not must now go to the Ministerio de Culturas and stand in line just to get a number to return later that night or the next day. My understanding from talking to other tour guides is that tour guides used to be able to buy your tickets and they would buy at the Peruvian National price and resell at the international tourist price. To cut down on this corruption now everyone must go and present their passports or their proof of residency (DNI). You are asked how many people in your party and you are given individual numbers for each person. You cannot show up early as they go by the number you were given. Their computer systems are not very stable so expect delays. We were in line a little over 2 hours in the morning and over 3 hours in the evening so almost 6 hours. When your number is called, you go upstairs and have to wait in and move yourself along the winding chairs that lead you back down the stairs to the cashier windows to be asked what circuit you will be getting. There are limited number of tickets per circuit and even though there are “maps” there is nothing showing you what you will see in each circuit and what your pictures will look like. There are screens that can be looked to determine which circuits are still available as you approach the window to place your order. Fee per permit/ticket is $50/person for international traveler regardless of the circuit chosen. Circuit 2A/B is quite popular so for me, I had to go with circuit 3A and that’s the picture I am sharing on this review. Previously on my other 2 visits, I was able to do the whole trip as a day trip and see Machu Picchu and return to Cusco the same day. Now you must stay overnight and some people up to two nights so plan carefully. That said, this area is a lower altitude than Cusco so it is easier to breathe and the town is much more quaint and more enjoyable. That said do not buy your return tickets to Cusco until you have your tour time because you are only allowed to make changes up to 24 hours before and if you try to change at 23 hours and 59 minutes, you will have to buy new tickets. Another piece of advice, when booking bus/train tickets go with Peru Rail instead of Inca Rail as the train cars are much nicer and have wider aisles and better “shows”. With these changes, it almost makes it not worth it to even visit. To me, Machu Picchu is completely overrated and overpriced and inconvenient to get to. Although I’m glad I’ve come 3 times (first time 2021 during covid and raining so pictures were not adequate, second time 2023 with niece and third time 2025 with my wife because she wanted to visit), I’ve thought this every time I’ve come.

It was nice, not even close to my favorite thing in Peru or my favorite trek but I would say definitely worth two to three days if you have time and are in the arequipa area. It is a very developed area for tourism so expect it to be busy and to have to pay for camping. I was recommended to take 5 litres of water, I took 6, and I did drink around 4, the reason I am included this however is that there were multiple locations where I could have refilled with my filter but didn't need to, therefore unless you drink a very large amount of water I wouldn't bring 6 litres. This is of course in the part of the canyon I did which started near cabanaconde.

A wonder del mundo, Machu Picchu is worth the long journey (flight to Lima, another flight to Cusco, taxi ride to Ollantaytambo, train ride to Aguas Calientes, and sunrise bus up to the entrance of Machu Picchu). Note that there are many prohibito items and there will be multiple pasaporte and boleto checks at the entrance and near the beginning of the path. Circuito 2 with the Inca bridge has an additional ranger patrolling the trail to the bridge, with a sign-in and sign-out process to control the number of visitors in the narrow area. A guide is technically mandatory for the entire park but is not rigidly enforced. However, it is recommended to arrange a trusted guia from the hotel rather than randomly picking one at the parada de autobus or entrada. My private guide charged 150 soles total for two people ($40 USD) and spent 3.5 hours from meeting at my hotel until leaving us at the area close to the salida (allowing us to stay and watch the llamas). Guides can help to take pictures of their clients at prime spots while explaining key details of the archeological history. Other than Inca bridge, there is no enforcement of the 4-hour total time limit through the complete circuit, though technically there is a one-way direction. Beware of biting insects (I washed my hands after applying a spray at the hotel and I received multiple bites from sand flies). The temperature is cool before sunrise but once the sun breaks through the fog and the view of the mountain is clear, the sunlight is bright and powerful. It's important to dress in layers and watch every step!

You need to arrive to santa teresa then to the electric station and then walk like 8 km to the entrace and then more like 1.5 km to the top of machu picchu. Buy tickets before or you will need to go the agua calientes and buy there(more 40 minuts walk). Total trip taked us 20 km.

1) YOU CANNOT WALK BACK, OR REPEAT ANY PART OF YOUR CIRCUIT. There are staff watching and they will blow their whistle and shout at you if you try. We arrived in a downpour so we walked to some shelter and waited it out. When we tried to walk back on the circuit we had paid for, we were not allowed. It doesn't matter that it's not busy, you want to take photos or any other reason, they will not let you walk back, even 20m. Unfortunately this was not clear when we purchased the ticket so we missed a significant portion of our route :( Therefore I'd advise you to walk slowly and enjoy the views, because once you arrive at the exit, that will be the end of your time at macchu pichu. I feel the staff lack compassion since it was 7am and quiet at macchu pichu, and I didn't want it to rain. If they had a cafe inside at the beginning I'd have waited there, but you have to enter at your time slot so I was left in a downpour in my poncho. I'd paid for flights, accomodation, and so many buses and trains to be there. The staff didn't show any compassion for my situation and were curt. <- therefore one star, but then I added one star for the staff that let me go take pictures of the llamas :) 2) TOILETS WILL COST YOU EXTRA ON TOP OF THE ENTRANCE FEE, AND BAGGAGE STORAGE. Which I also find ridiculous considering the entrance ticket is so expensive. Toilet S/2 and baggage is S/5. 3) YOU CANNOT ENTER BEFORE THE ENTRANCE TIME ON YOUR TICKET Something to do with the system they use. 4) DO NOT FLY A DRONE The staff have a walkie talkie system and respond very quickly. I don't know what the consequences are, but the people we saw were taken to the office. 5) DO NOT BOOK EARLY MORNING TIME SLOT IF YOU ARE VISITING IN WET/ SHOULDER SEASONS The clouds tend to hang low in the sky in the morning so you might not have the best view/ you might have to wait for clouds to clear. 6) YOU CAN BUY THE BUS TICKET FOR THE BUS UPWARDS ONLINE, BEFOREHAND (12 USD per ride) Just look for the one bus company that runs the route. I can't remember off the top of my head. You can also purchase them at the bottom and at the top, the process is quick and easy. Hope this helps :) I did route 1&2 and was happy with all the areas it covered.

El cañon del colca tiene un paisaje imponente e inmenso, mucha flora y fauna. Hay que ir con buen calzado y un nivel intermedio de estado físico ya que hay que hacer trekking en altura y resulta medio dificultoso si no estás acostumbrado. Es viaje es bastante extenso, más o menos son 2 o 3 horas para llegar. No tuve la suerte de ver ningún Cóndor pero fue una linda experiencia. Esta excursión no la recomiendo hacer si vas a Perú con los días contados, hay lugares mejores.

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Hoy descubrí la historia del señor de sipán El museo un amplio complejo muy agradable con una zona trasera con casetas de artesanos La entrada se paga al entrar al recinto, para meter el coche son dos soles y la entrada de adultos son 10 soles No se puede acceder ni con móviles, cámaras ni bolsos al museo. Las taquillas las tienen en el exterior( cosa poco usual y de primeras un poco desconcertante, en fin) Puedes pagar por un guia 30soles. El museo se divide en tres plantas, no es muy grande se ve en una hora aproximadamente. Lo peor que vi es la oscuridad que tiene el museo. Las vitrinas con texto no se puede leer bien.

A decir verdad, la arquitectura es imponente, sus construcciones y su arquitectura antigua y diseño. Ahora bien, lamentablemente tengo que decir que estaba todo cercado, lo cual no pudimos tener acceso a la plaza. Cercado y con policías que no dejaban que uno pueda caminar siquiera la vereda de la plaza. A su vez, la catedral también permanecía cerrada y fue muy decepcionante. Por lo que fue ir mirar de lejos y volver.. una visita media mala, y que no recomiendo en absoluto. Gastas más en uber que en lo que podes disfrutar jajajajajja

A few people have compared this to Machu Pichu, but this is odd and inaccurate, these ruins should be approached on their own merit. That said, I can't fully recommend heading this far into the highlands just to visit this site - it is certainly interesting, quiet and something different, not an essential visit on a travel schedule though.

Si puedes ve con un guía, ellos te explican la historia del monasterio de una manera muy dinámica (la guía Claudia es muy buena relatando). Es un lugar muy pintoresco por los colores de las calles, la arquitectura es curiosa, el arte hermoso y la vista desde la cúpula es genial. Dato para los estudiantes, lleven su carnet universitario para pagar la entrada de 12 soles. Adentro venden artesanías desde joyería hasta una crema de pétalos de rosa preparada por las monjas. Además cuentan con una cafetería con precios un poco alto.

15 soles entry. Some good production value has been out into the exhibition room with atmospheric music and cool artifacts with good explanations in English. The tour itself was great and our tour guide had great English (included in the ticket price). He explained about not only the archaeological site itself but showed us Peruvian/Liman fruit, vegetables and animals. Some good examples set up with mannequins to explain the way it was built and about their culture.

Una advertencia muy seria y real es que acá casi todo turista que visita Perú llega a este sitio que no es nada barato empezando con lo absurdamente caro que es el Tren que es el único medio (práctico y rápido) para llegar a Aguascalientes cuyo pasaje de ida y vuelta fácilmente te gastas 160 dólares, la entrada se tiene que comprar en línea con al menos 40 días de anticipación y el transporte de subida y bajada a las ruinas cuesta 24 dólares, no se gastarán menos de 220 dólares, lo terrible de esto es que el lugar siempre está atestado de gente se hacen largas filas para comprar pasajes de el bus, para subirse en el para regresarse, fila para entrar y ahí se te pueden ir fácilmente 4 horas si 4 horas, el clima es traiciónero en cualquier momento llueve y te frusta meses de planeación, por otro lados la Cuidad Perdida es simplemente subreal 😍

Las telecabinas son de última generación, es un paseo bastante interesante. Pero la fortaleza está aún muy lejos de ser un punto turístico como Machu Picchu. Hay que caminar sobre tierra, rocas y lodo para poder llegar a la cima en donde verán la fortaleza desde afuera porque no se puede ingresar al complejo. La caminata dura aproximadamente 2 horas y es muy difícil para niños y adultos mayores, incluso personas que no tienen mucha resistencia física.
Maravillosa naturaleza. Ideal para caminar (a pesar de la altura) y disfrutar del paisaje.

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Imperdible visita si haces turismo en Chiclayo. Cerca del centro de la ciudad, estacionamiento y bastante orden. Si deseas conocer a fondo la historia de los diferentes señores que gobernaron está región en la época precolombina, es necesario contratar el servicio de un guía, disponibles al ingreso. Podrás ver todo tipo de piezas desde vasijas hasta impresionantes piezas de oro. Además replicas de las tumbas encontradas, entre otras maravillas. Digno de ver al menos una vez en la vida

My rating and comments are not related to the Machu Picchu itself (which is definitely one of the wonder of the world) but by the way visits are organized. It has unfortunately turned into a tourist zoo -it’s seem to be more daily tourists than the theoretical 2500 tickets allowance -visit must follow a one way buckle path, forget about following your inspiration to walk around -you must cross and queue within an horrible and crowded tourist village located around the train station -be also ready to queue within train station, to get on board the bus to go down, to enter the sight... -and obviously to contribute generously to Peruvian government budget

Se nota que devastaron el lugar, pues la ciudad lo fue arruinando. Fue algo decepcionante ver obreros refaccionando la pirámide cual antiguos creadores de la misma

text_clean: We loved it. Went to Pisco airport (local airport), took about 50 min to get to site, then the roller coaster ride began. They bank turn, bank left to allow passengers on both sides to see it. We went with Aerodiana, nice plane in good condition. Lucky I read about the other unsafe airlines and emergency landings after my ride or would have been a nervous wreck during the flight. Was lucky had a beautiful sunny clear day and smooth ride (aside from the purpose banking, poor kid on our plane threw up. The plane had plenty of bags for situations like these 😂)

El lugar no tiene facilidades para personas con movilidad limitada. Adicionalmente, una guía llamada Saskia Prieto fue discriminante con una turista porque la turista no pudo caminar a la velocidad de su grupo y se retiró para poder avanzar sola a su propio paso. La turista fue maltratada y sin respetar sus derechos ni sus limitaciones esta mala guía, obligó a la turista a salir del lugar humillándola! Es un acto discriminatorio! Será que las personas de movilidad limitada no tenemos derecho para visitar este lugar histórico???

Al igual que los dibujos de los campos de todo el mundo hechos por seres un poquito mas matematicos y creativos que nosotros Las lineas de Nazca son un juego La estacion del mono o del colibri Los aliens son muy ludicos su hermosa vida superior lo hace asi Otra prueba desu origen se puede ver en el mar donde con gogle se puede ver que tambien las hicieron alli Arq.Raúl Cordero Ecuador

Arequipa is an incredible experience overall and surprisingly affordable! Known as the White City, Arequipa boasts charming architecture and a vibrant culture that makes every visit special. One of the highlights of my trip was visiting the breathtaking Colca Canyon. The landscapes of Colca Canyon are absolutely unbelievable. The vast, rugged beauty of the canyon leaves you in awe, and it's a fantastic place to connect with nature. The tranquility and peacefulness you find there are unmatched. It's a perfect escape from the hustle and bustle of daily life. Walking along the canyon, you can truly appreciate the serene environment and the stunning views that stretch as far as the eye can see. Watching the majestic condors glide through the air is an experience in itself. Arequipa adds to the experience with its unique blend of historical sites and modern amenities. The combination of the city's unique charm and the breathtaking beauty of Colca Canyon makes for a memorable visit. Whether you're hiking the trails, soaking in the hot springs, or simply taking in the views, it's a place that invites you to relax and unwind. Overall, visiting Colca Canyon in Arequipa is an unforgettable experience that I highly recommend. The natural beauty, peaceful atmosphere, and affordability make it a must-see destination for anyone looking to explore the wonders of Peru.

Cuando este parque se creó incluía poder disfrutar de todas las piletas por una sola entrada. El alcalde que lo diseñó pensó en el relajo de la rutina y un regalo a la población. Ahora cobran doble por ver todas las piletas con la excusa de hacer un espectáculo temático. Aparte de ser terrible, al haber quitado áreas verdes para poner insoportables juegos desmerece la obra. Qué tal desproposito cumple esta obra ahora. Espero puedan considerar regresar el diseño del parque a sus orígenes.

Fui al circuito 1-C el 17 de abril a las 8:00 am y me tocó lluvia y niebla. No obstante despeja por momentos y se puede apreciar la vista desde la casa del guardian al que se tiene acceso, pero por la condición climática descarté la subida que ofrecía la ruta porque no valía la pena. Mejor reservar para entrar al medio día, vas a la fija. Aunque el paisaje es encantador por las montañas, incluso desde el tren, me pareció que tanto sacrificio y dinero para llegar hasta allí está un poco sobrevalorado. Demasiados turistas.

Realmente muy costoso. Subir en bus en precio para latinos es un abuso, para un tramo de 20 minutos. Les recomiendo lleguuen muy antes a cusco y suban caminando. Una vez arriba es bonita la vista pero los guardias dentro del lugar son GROSEROS, ALTANEROS. Vivi una pesima experiencia gracias a ellos. Recuerdo que con mis compañeros hicimos un tiktok donde nos veíamos emocionados de estar alla. Y llego un tipo de estos diciendo que borraramos el video que me iba a decomisar el celular. Que esta prohibido grabar videos unas cosas super estupidas. Hicieron de la estancia una pésima experiencia. … no pagaría jamas por volver. Hay lugares mil veces mejores y mil veces mas economicos y sin estos tipos.

Machu Picchu itself is certainly an impressive archeological site. The price (entry ticket per circuit 152S, resp. 50$) is very high in my regards, especially since there is zero information given on the site itself. Further all means of transport are overpriced compared to the rest of Peru and there is no alternative. Overall a nice experience, but felt overpriced, without any reasonable alternative for means of transport, too little/no information and thus a overrated.

Una de las maravillas del mundo Antiguo Machu Picchu es una de las joyas arqueológicas mas espectaculares del mundo 🤩. La energía de este lugar es increíble 😊 Recomiendo: - Hacer las reservaciones para las 11:00 ya que es una hora estratégica y no hay mucha aglomeración de personas y el sol no es tan intenso. ☀️ - Llevar mucha agua 💦 porque el recorrido es largo y el calor es intenso🥵 - Llevar bloqueador solar 🧴 ☀️ - Obligatoriamente llevar repelente de insectos 🦟 ya qué hay muchos la mosquitos 🦟 y sus picaduras son muy dolorosas. - Llevar un poncho de lluvia 🌧 - Después del recorrido tomate un Cuzqueña 🍻

Dependiendo que tanto demoren en ver cada lugar y su ritmo de caminata, el ticket solo permite 3 horas de visita ya que si no, tendrán que pagar de nuevo el ingreso a telecabina, el tiempo de visita a la ciudadela es ilimitado pero estar atento al ticket de la telecabina.

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Un museo muy bonito, sin embargo es muy frustrante que no te dejen tomar fotos dentro de este. Tiene tres pisos, empiezas por el tercero y vas bajando, te muestran no sólo la tumba del señor de sipan, sino también otras tumbas que se encontraron dentro de la misma huaca, sus métodos de sepultura, la cerámica de los mochica y su cosmovisión (dioses).

We weren't sure if it would fit in our schedule for Peru at first, but I'm very glad we made a detour and planned some extra time for this. Absolutely stunning views and very varied flora and fauna. A few notes: the local authority Autocolca doesn't do their bookkeeping very well and I don't recommend getting E-tickets. Get your tickets in the early morning from their office in Chivay (plaza des armas) as you have the best chance of spotting the mighty condor in the morning, and they don't do much work there in the afternoon. Also I do not recommend going over the S109 (south south west) unless you have plenty of time (travel time with google maps is spot-on though) a sturdy car and good driving skills, because the road is undergoing about 100kms of heavy construction with poor driving conditions.
