Arequipa is an incredible experience overall and surprisingly affordable! Known as the White City, Arequipa boasts charming architecture and a vibrant culture that makes every visit special. One of the highlights of my trip was visiting the breathtaking Colca Canyon. The landscapes of Colca Canyon are absolutely unbelievable. The vast, rugged beauty of the canyon leaves you in awe, and it's a fantastic place to connect with nature. The tranquility and peacefulness you find there are unmatched. It's a perfect escape from the hustle and bustle of daily life. Walking along the canyon, you can truly appreciate the serene environment and the stunning views that stretch as far as the eye can see. Cuando este parque se creó incluía poder disfrutar de todas las piletas por una sola entrada. El alcalde que lo diseñó pensó en el relajo de la rutina y un regalo a la población. Ahora cobran doble por ver todas las piletas con la excusa de hacer un espectáculo temático. Aparte de ser terrible, al haber quitado áreas verdes para poner insoportables juegos desmerece la obra. Qué tal desproposito cumple esta obra ahora. Espero puedan considerar regresar el diseño del parque a sus orígenes.

Fui al circuito 1-C el 17 de abril a las 8:00 am y me tocó lluvia y niebla. No obstante despeja por momentos y se puede apreciar la vista desde la casa del guardian al que se tiene acceso, pero por la condición climática descarté la subida que ofrecía la ruta porque no valía la pena. Mejor reservar para entrar al medio día, vas a la fija. Aunque el paisaje es encantador por las montañas, incluso desde el tren, me pareció que tanto sacrificio y dinero para llegar hasta allí está un poco sobrevalorado. Demasiados turistas.

Una de las siete maravillas del mundo, sin duda un lugar espectacular y majestuoso con mucha historia y arquitectura, paisajes y vistas únicas, este lugar está dividido por circuitos, el que tiene mejor vista a la ciudadela es el número dos, recomendable compras las entradas con anticipación ya que hay mucha afluencia de turistas y es difícil adquirir las entradas de un día para otro.

Realmente muy costoso. Subir en bus en precio para latinos es un abuso, para un tramo de 20 minutos. Les recomiendo lleguuen muy antes a cusco y suban caminando. Una vez arriba es bonita la vista pero los guardias dentro del lugar son GROSEROS, ALTANEROS. Vivi una pesima experiencia gracias a ellos. Recuerdo que con mis compañeros hicimos un tiktok donde nos veíamos emocionados de estar alla. Y llego un tipo de estos diciendo que borraramos el video que me iba a decomisar el celular. Que esta prohibido grabar videos unas cosas super estupidas. Hicieron de la estancia una pésima experiencia. … no pagaría jamas por volver. Hay lugares mil veces mejores y mil veces mas economicos y sin estos tipos.

Machu Picchu itself is certainly an impressive archeological site. The price (entry ticket per circuit 152S, resp. 50$) is very high in my regards, especially since there is zero information given on the site itself. Further all means of transport are overpriced compared to the rest of Peru and there is no alternative. Overall a nice experience, but felt overpriced, without any reasonable alternative for means of transport, too little/no information and thus a overrated.

Una de las maravillas del mundo Antiguo Machu Picchu es una de las joyas arqueológicas mas espectaculares del mundo 🤩. La energía de este lugar es increíble 😊 Recomiendo: - Hacer las reservaciones para las 11:00 ya que es una hora estratégica y no hay mucha aglomeración de personas y el sol no es tan intenso. ☀️ - Llevar mucha agua 💦 porque el recorrido es largo y el calor es intenso🥵 - Llevar bloqueador solar 🧴 ☀️ - Obligatoriamente llevar repelente de insectos 🦟 ya qué hay muchos la mosquitos 🦟 y sus picaduras son muy dolorosas. - Llevar un poncho de lluvia 🌧 - Después del recorrido tomate un Cuzqueña 🍻

Lugar muy interesante, aun falta más apoyo y trabajo de reparación de la ciudadela, el circuito es con bastantes escaleras y subidas, tener cuidado con los pisos de madera del circuito ya que se pueden tropezar, el tiempo de recorrido es de 2 a 3 horas aprox. Dependiendo que tanto demoren en ver cada lugar y su ritmo de caminata, el ticket solo permite 3 horas de visita ya que si no, tendrán que pagar de nuevo el ingreso a telecabina, el tiempo de visita a la ciudadela es ilimitado pero estar atento al ticket de la telecabina.

Al principio dudamos si pagar la entrada o no. Pero al final valió la pena pagar el acceso y recorrer todo el monasterio. Estuvimos un par de horas visitando cada estancia de este lugar, a cual nos sorprendió más. Lo tienen muy bien conservado, tiene fácil acceso y todo bien indicado. Cuenta con baños por varios puntos del monasterio, un bar y alguna tienda. Fuimos un sábado sobre las 10:00 y no había mucha gente, pero igualmente al ser tan grande no hay aglomeraciones. Muy recomendable

destination: Museo Tumbas Reales del Señor de Sipán
text_clean: Un museo muy bonito, sin embargo es muy frustrante que no te dejen tomar fotos dentro de este. Tiene tres pisos, empiezas por el tercero y vas bajando, te muestran no sólo la tumba del señor de sipan, sino también otras tumbas que se encontraron dentro de la misma huaca, sus métodos de sepultura, la cerámica de los mochica y su cosmovisión (dioses).

We weren't sure if it would fit in our schedule for Peru at first, but I'm very glad we made a detour and planned some extra time for this. Absolutely stunning views and very varied flora and fauna. A few notes: the local authority Autocolca doesn't do their bookkeeping very well and I don't recommend getting E-tickets. Get your tickets in the early morning from their office in Chivay (plaza des armas) as you have the best chance of spotting the mighty condor in the morning, and they don't do much work there in the afternoon. Also I do not recommend going over the S109 (south south west) unless you have plenty of time (travel time with google maps is spot-on though) a sturdy car and good driving skills, because the road is undergoing about 100kms of heavy construction with poor driving conditions.

I came to Arequipa to enjoy the city and to visit the Monastery was something I was excited about doing. I arrived yesterday and I was extremely disappointed to find that there was no way for someone like me to enter and enjoy this beautiful piece of architecture and history.. I have muscular dystrophy and to use stairs is basically impossible for me. I understand that it is a historic city and things sometimes can’t be changed for preservation purposes - but to have no ramp or mini platform elevator or alternate entrance or even a handrail for the stairs was shocking and disappointing to say the least. I only write this review to let others know who may be physically altered (senior citizens or pregnant women) or handicapped like me that this will be what you find when you arrive there. It makes no sense for a place that is so historical and beautiful and important, a tourist treasure of the city, to not be accesible to everyone. I was still able to enjoy some of the beautiful architecture from the outside, but it was very sad not to be able to experience this.

Disappointing :-( First of all, my experience has nothing to do with the actual site. The view, ruins, landscape and llamas are surreal! My disappointing experience is about the management of the site. I knew it was a touristy place and quite expensive to go. Most places are restricted/controlled and you can't enter inside the temples etc..you can just go around. Apparently a few years ago everything was open (said some friends who went before). And then you have all the tour groups that take a lot of spaces on the path. I almost didn't take any pictures, I didn't feel I had the freedom to do so because the path goes only on one way (so you can't go back on your path) and people are moving so you kind of feel a bit in a tour group without being in it and you kind of have to go forward. I didn't feel free to walk around/take pictures as I would have liked to. And then there are 4 choices of tickets, with different circuits, bit it doesn't give you access to everywhere with only one ticket, so you need to buy another one. This wasn't very cleared on the website. I just find that you put so much effort (physically, mentally and financially) to get there (waking up super early, doing all the trip to get to Aguas Calientes, walking up the (many!) stairs to the entrance, and the cost etc), in the end to leave with a bitter feeling. Is it worth it? I'm still unsure if it was.

I really hate 1 star reviews for this historical site but I am writing one. Due to the protests in September of 2025 we were unable to get our train to Aguas Caliente. I completely support people's right to protest and was unwilling to travel to MP during this dispute. Some people travelled by van for 5 hours and walked for several more miles to access the town. I chose to stay in Ollantaytambo for a couple of extra days which was really enjoyable. I am giving MP 1 star because despite the issues people have accessing MP their refund process is impossible. I was told I need to submit personal information including pictures of my passport. They sent me a link to do this. The link does not say anything about the purpose of why I am submitting this information. I am wary of providing this information without a guarantee of getting a refund. The ticketing website has no information about asking for refunds. Surely they are aware that thousands of people were unable to go to MP due to the strike. Perurail actually sent an email explaining the refund process. I imagine the ministry of tourism made quite a bit of money from no-show tickets.

Perurail actually sent an email explaining the refund process. I imagine the ministry of tourism made quite a bit of money from no-show tickets.
